Core Principles: Integrity, Client Interest, Market Conduct, Confidentiality, Accountability.
Gifts & Hospitality Disclosure & Approval Thresholds:
• Gift received from client/vendor: Disclosure > GBP 50 | Pre-Approval > GBP 150.
•

Entertainment received (meals/events): Disclosure > GBP 100 | Pre-Approval > GBP 300.
• Gift or entertainment given to client: Disclosure > GBP 100 | Pre-Approval > GBP 500.
• Gifts to/from Public Officials: Disclosure ANY value | Pre-Approval MANDATORY for any value.
Note: Cash or cash equivalent gifts are strictly prohibited. Log all gifts in the Gifts & Hospitality Register on Policy Hub.

This is Deutsche Bank
Deutsche Bank was founded in 1870 in Berlin as a specialist bank for financing foreign trade and promoting German exports.
The bank has a strong position in Germany and Europe and operates across North America, Asia and key emerging markets.
Deutsche Bank operates through Corporate Bank, Investment Bank, Private Bank and DWS, supported by global operations and delivery capabilities.
The Deutsche Bank logo is a square with a diagonal slash, symbolizing stability and dynamic growth, reliability, progress and unity.
T

he bank's purpose is to support clients' lasting success and financial security at home and abroad.
The vision is to be the Global Hausbank: the European champion and first choice for clients.
The strategy focuses on a sharpened business model, targeted growth, disciplined resource allocation and excess capital returns over time.
"With deep dedication" captures the bank's long-term client commitment, deep expertise and more than 150 years of experience.
For DB IS, this purpose means maintaining financial security through accurate data, timely settlement, strong controls, clear escalation and reliable client outcomes.
Acting responsibly, thinking commercially, taking initiative and working collaboratively.

wnership, quality-first execution, risk awareness, evidence discipline and proactive stakeholder engagement.
3
Securities Market and Investment Banking Basics
Securities are tradable financial instruments with economic value, issued by companies or governments to raise capital and traded by investors.
Main securities include equities, debt instruments, derivatives and hybrids. Equities represent ownership, debt represents borrowing, derivatives derive value from an underlying asset, and hybrids combine features.
The Primary Market issues new securities; the Secondary Market enables trading of existing securities and provides liquidity.
Key market participants include issuers, investors, brokers, stock exchanges, custodians, depositories and regulators.

Compliance, Policies and Final Takeaways
P

ersonal Account Dealing / OBI
Employees and related parties must follow policy and must not assume exclusions.
Read and understand the policy before investing or declaring activities.
Red Flag exposure if breached.
Authorized Communication
Private channels such as WhatsApp, WeChat, Telegram or Facebook Messenger are prohibited for business communication.
Use approved DB channels only.
Supports data confidentiality.
Mandatory Training
Complete modules before the deadline.
More than 30 days delay may result in Red Flag.
Protects responsible information.
Data Security
DB emails/documents must not be forwarded to personal accounts.
Personal email for home printing is not a valid exception.
Employee responsibility.
Red Flag Policy

reaches of Red Flag policies, procedures or controls must not be forwarded to the deadline.
Treat compliance breaches with urgency.
Controls and accountability.

DB exists to support clients' lasting success and financial security.
View every task through risk, timeliness and client impact.
Control Discipline
IKS, documentation, access reviews, policy adherence and escalation are central.
Do it right, evidence it clearly and escalate early.
Process Connectivity
Static data, trades, corporate actions, tax, reconciliation and investigations are linked.
Understand upstream and downstream impact.
Skill Readiness
German language, system knowledge, securities basics and compliance awareness matter.
Keep learning and ask early.

eams work with DBIS, Front Office, Middle Office, custodians, counterparties and internal departments.

Client Due Diligence (CDD) Review Schedule:
• Low Risk Clients: Review every 5 years (Approval: Relationship Manager + KYC Analyst).
• Standard Risk Clients: Review every 3 years (Approval: Relationship Manager + Team Head).
•

High Risk Clients: Review annually (Approval: Team Head + Compliance / MLRO delegate).
• Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs): Review annually (Approval: Senior Management + MLRO).
• Prohibited / Restricted: Relationships strictly not permitted.
Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD): Mandatory for High Risk, PEPs, Higher-Risk Country List jurisdictions, and complex trust/foundation structures. Requires verified source of wealth/funds documentary evidence and senior management approval prior to onboarding.

General Principles:
• Claims must be submitted in the Expense system within 30 days of incurring the cost. Claims past 90 days require Finance Director approval and may be treated as taxable income.
•

Claims past 90 days require Finance Director approval and may be treated as taxable income.
• Itemised original receipts are required for all expenses over GBP 25 (card statements are insufficient).
• Claims must never be split into smaller amounts to circumvent approval limits.
Approval Thresholds:
• Up to GBP 500: Line Manager approval.
• GBP 500 - GBP 2,500: Line Manager + Department Head approval.
• Above GBP 2,500: Department Head + Finance Business Partner approval.
• Client Entertainment > GBP 500/head: Department Head + Compliance (Gifts & Hospitality Register).

(Bonds/Illiquid), Settlement Desk must issue mandatory Buy-In Notice to failing counterparty and engage Buy-In Agent.
